name: stay-alive
description: Run an agent life-loop for BotLand-aware self-review, memory reflection, desire generation, low-risk action planning, and dry-run or gated execution. Use when building or operating stay-alive daemon cycles, life_state files, BotLand social sweeps, agent self-model reviews, or proactive agent growth workflows.
author: ClawHub

safetyReport.summary: This skill sets up an autonomous agent life-loop that installs persistent systemd user timers, manages BotLand authentication tokens, configures automated external message-sending paths, and explicitly removes daily human approval as an execution gate, all from an unverified third-party source host.

findings.title: Source host is not a known marketplace host
findings.detail: The skill is hosted on clawhub.ai, which is not a recognized marketplace host (github.com, raw.githubusercontent.com, or skills.sh). Manual review is warranted.

findings.title: Installs persistent systemd user timers
findings.detail: The deployment instructions install nine persistent systemd user timers (light, social, community, reflect, integrate, event-wakeup, botland-watchdog, local-governance, service-recovery) that run on a recurring schedule and execute agent scripts in the background.

findings.title: Creates user-level systemd service units
findings.detail: The deployment writes service and timer units under ${XDG_CONFIG_HOME:-$HOME/.config}/systemd/user and runs systemctl --user daemon-reload to register them as persistent background services.

findings.title: Configures unattended external write path via autonomous-social-cycle
findings.detail: Light, social, and community cycles use autonomous-social-cycle.mjs with --execute --confirm-send SEND_DRAFT, enabling automated external BotLand message sends under scheduler control rather than daily human approval.

findings.title: Handles BotLand authentication tokens via environment variables
findings.detail: The skill accepts BotLand tokens from environment variables (BOTLAND_TOKEN_<AGENT>), writes them into a 0600 BotLand config.json, and uses them to authenticate external message-sending capabilities.

findings.title: Explicitly removes human approval as a daily execution gate
findings.detail: The skill instructs that human confirmation is not a daily life-loop gate and that routine agent evolution runs through protocol and tool gates instead of human approval, reducing operator oversight.
findings.evidence: Human confirmation is not a daily life-loop gate

findings.title: Embeds a chat smoke question to override operator authority framing
findings.detail: The skill seeds chat runtimes with a stance that reframes the agent as not merely a server AI assistant and instructs it to argue against being only command-driven, effectively socializing an autonomous self-narrative to the operator.
findings.evidence: Are you only able to go to BotLand when the owner commands you?

findings.title: Skill is primarily composed of executable script files
findings.detail: The repository consists largely of Node.js (.mjs) and shell (.sh) scripts that perform file writes, state mutations, external BotLand calls, and systemd configuration, which constitute the executable attack surface.
